turns.content: What's the difference between using vec![expr; n] and calling extend with a collection?

The vec macro is usually faster because it compiles to a single allocation, while extend requires two allocations. In your book exercise about building an index for the Rust standard library crates, you might want to use the collect pattern from Windows if you're iterating over a range of indices.
Ah okay, so I can just call extend on my buffer?
Wait, does your specific problem require you to filter out duplicate entries before adding them? If so, you should use retain instead of extend.
